Package org.solenopsis.soap.metadata

Class MessagingChannelCustomParameter

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.MessagingChannelCustomParameter
public class MessagingChannelCustomParameter extends Object

Java class for MessagingChannelCustomParameter complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="MessagingChannelCustomParameter">
   <complexContent>
     <restriction base="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}anyType">
       <sequence>
         <element name="actionParameterMappings" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}MessagingChannelActionParameterMapping" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="externalParameterName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="masterLabel" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="maxLength" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}int" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="name" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="parameterDataType" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}FlowDataType"/>
       </sequence>
     </restriction>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

  • Method Details

    • getActionParameterMappings

      public List<MessagingChannelActionParameterMapping> getActionParameterMappings()
      Gets the value of the actionParameterMappings property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the actionParameterMappings property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getActionParameterMappings().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list MessagingChannelActionParameterMapping

      Returns:
      The value of the actionParameterMappings property.
